text:
Once there was a man who lived in a cozy little cottage overlooking a vibrant forest. He loved the wood sprites there - their sparkling feathers, their lively music.

One night, as the stars twinkled down like diamonds, the man dreamed of magic. He envisioned a forest fair where these sprites could dance every evening. The first day they summoned him, he was thrilled.

There were seven beauties beside him. They twirled and jumped, their laughter like music pouring through the trees. The man danced too. They showed him secrets of magic - growing flowers that sang, casting invisible nets that caught butterflies. When it was time to leave, they gave him a sparkling feathered cap. They said it'd shield him from harm.

He wore it every day. Years passed, and no sprites showed up. People whispered that maybe the legend was just that. Still, on weekends, he'd peek out his window, hoping...

One afternoon a shadowy figure slipped through the trees. The sprite cap
